description_long: This is a small, single-propeller military training airplane with a streamlined fuselage and low-mounted straight wings. The aircraft is painted primarily white with bold red accents on the nose, tail, and wingtips, and features black striping along the top of the fuselage. Distinctive U.S. Navy insignia and identification numbers are visible on the fuselage and wings, indicating its military use. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy and appears to be configured for a single pilot. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all accented in red. The landing gear is fixed and tricycle in configuration, with visible wheels under the nose and main fuselage.
description: A small, single-propeller U.S. Navy training airplane with white, red, and black markings, low straight wings, and a fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Left side view showing the U.S. Navy insignia, red tail, and red-tipped wings, with the propeller and cockpit canopy visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the single propeller, symmetrical low wings with red tips, and the narrow fuselage profile.
description_view_2: Right side view displaying the aircraft's identification numbers, red tail, and the full length of the fuselage and wings.
description_view_3: Head-on view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the straight, low-mounted wings with red tips.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
